body.u: In July 1978, I left the Sheppey comprehensive and in September I began a technician apprentershipapprenticeship at Marconi-Elliots. After being trained for several years, I started my job properly at their factory at RotchesterRochester airport. It was at this time that I left home took up lodgings nearer my work. The owners were a middle aged couple whose eldest child was now in the sixth form of this school. The husband was a short stout man who was unemployed at the time, and was finding it hard to get a job because of his age. His wife, also short and stout, had near to grey hair and suffered from diabeitisdiabetes.

The youngest son, aged fifteen was approaching his exams and I was able to help him on subjects that I had taken myself. The older brother who was taking ‘A’ levels did not need any help and as I only took ‘O’ levels, I could not help him much anyway.

At the age of twenty nine, my firm opened up a factory in my home town of Sheerness, and I was sent there and also promoted to foreman. I moved again and took residence in a new block of flats near to my parentsparents' home.

Three years later, I did something I had always wanted to do, become self-employed. I was a mechanic, and put an addad in the local newspaper saying that I would make house calls to any customers. This proved a success so after only six months I began to hire a place and run a garage, which also went ofoff to a good start. It was about this time when I met a girl that had known at school, after several months of dating each other we got married. I moved out of the flat I had lived in, and together we brought a house in Minster, a short distance from the garage.
